what_it_does: A self-contained browser IIFE that listens for gallery:rendered and decision:rendered events, walks the rendered DOM root, attaches a small annotation UI strip to each addressable element, and emits user actions (comments, reactions, favours, and decision takes) as CustomEvents so downstream code can route them by address.
observations: Defines REACTIONS and CONTENT_COMMENT_TYPES arrays as the interaction vocabulary.
Uses a local _seq counter plus window.__galleryAnnSalt to generate annotation ids.
Factory functions comment, reaction, and favour produce mark objects carrying id, type, and element_id.
Emits gallery:verb with verb 'annotate' and also re-emits the legacy gallery:direction event as a transition alias.
Implements a strict shape guard for decision:// addresses and throws rather than emit a non-canonical address.
makeAnnotatable appends an .annotation-strip with comment, favour, and reaction buttons.
openCommentPicker renders one panel with type chips and a textarea, not per-type buttons.
markPlaced appends a .placed-stamp element for visual feedback.
Self-wires to both gallery:rendered and decision:rendered through the same _onRendered handler.
Derives element sub-addresses from the base address plus element id or a generated index.
Looks for detail.anchorableSelector first, then [data-anchorable], then a semantic HTML fallback selector.
Warns loudly when zero anchorable elements are found under a base address.
Exposes window.galleryBinder for explicit direct binding.
standouts: Dual event emission: gallery:verb envelope plus a no-break legacy gallery:direction alias.
Decision address canonical-shape guard that refuses to emit instead of reimplementing canonicalization.
Single handler wired to both gallery:rendered and decision:rendered events.
Address-driven binding contract where DNA declares anchorableSelector or tags [data-anchorable].
conventions: IIFE module pattern with a window global API.
data-* attributes for binding state and addresses.
Semantic HTML fallback for anchorable elements.
concerns: Uses window.prompt for favour input, which is blocking and unstyled.
Builds innerHTML strings from controlled constants; any change to make those dynamic could introduce injection risk.
Semantic fallback selector may bind zero elements on non-semantic markup, as the file itself acknowledges.
Throws synchronously in decide() on an invalid address, which could crash the caller if not caught.
Global mutable counters (_seq and window.__galleryAnnSalt) could reset or collide across reloads.
Maintains a legacy event alias that is intended to be removed later.
notes: Middle-of-circuit interaction binder: turns rendered gallery surfaces into addressable annotation targets and routes user marks out as events.
questions: Which component consumes gallery:verb and how is the verb dispatcher implemented?
What is the exact expected detail shape for decision:rendered beyond element/root/address?
When can the legacy gallery:direction alias be safely removed?
How are id collisions avoided when the same base address is re-rendered and semantic elements lack ids?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to sit between rendering and state-writing; the code matches that by binding UI and emitting events without rendering or persisting state.
apparent_themes: Contract-driven integration between renderer, binder, and state writer.
Backward compatibility during a transition period.
intention_signals: It is trying to provide a portable, reusable interaction layer that any gallery renderer can adopt by emitting a render event, while keeping annotation semantics stable and routing decision takes to the correct territory writer.
novelty: The explicit decision-address canonical-shape guard that refuses to emit rather than reimplement canonicalization in JS, and the gallery:verb unified envelope with a documented legacy no-break alias.
contribution: A reusable binding mechanism that attaches annotation controls to rendered DOM elements, normalizes user marks into address-tagged events, and exposes a small API for direct integration.
summary_for_embedding: wildcard-gallery-binder.js is a browser IIFE that wires annotation UI strips to rendered gallery elements by address and emits comments, reactions, favours, and decision takes as CustomEvents for downstream dispatch.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to serve as a portable interaction middle-layer between gallery rendering and fork/state-writing, letting any compliant renderer become annotatable without owning state or styling.

symbols.does: Generates a unique annotation identifier using a local sequence and a global salt.
symbols.description: annId increments a module-local counter and a window.__galleryAnnSalt value, then returns a string id of the form ann-<seq>-<salt>. It is used by the comment, reaction, and favour factories to give each mark a distinct id.

symbols.does: Factory that creates a comment mark object.
symbols.description: comment(addr, type, text) returns an object with id, type 'comment', annotation_type, text, and element_id set to the supplied address. It is one of the three canonical item factories.

symbols.does: Factory that creates a reaction mark object.
symbols.description: reaction(addr, r) returns an object with id, type 'reaction', reaction, and element_id set to the supplied address. The reaction value comes from the REACTIONS vocabulary.

symbols.does: Factory that creates a favour mark object.
symbols.description: favour(addr, score) returns an object with id, type 'favour', score, and element_id set to the supplied address. The score is typically a number entered through a prompt.

symbols.does: Dispatches a gallery:verb CustomEvent carrying a unified envelope.
symbols.description: emitVerb(verb, aim_address, payload) constructs a CustomEvent named 'gallery:verb' with detail {verb, aim_address, payload}. It is the central outbound routing function used by annotations and decision takes.

symbols.does: Converts an annotation item into a gallery:verb event and also emits a legacy gallery:direction event.
symbols.description: emitDirection(item) maps the item type to a mark_type payload, copies optional text, annotation_type, reaction, and score fields, calls emitVerb('annotate', item.element_id, payload), and then re-emits the same item as a 'gallery:direction' event for backward compatibility.

symbols.does: Validates that a decision address has the expected canonical shape.
symbols.description: _isCanonicalDecisionAddr(addr) checks that the address is a string starting with 'decision://' and matches one of the allowed frame patterns: global/<id>, project/<sid>/<id>, user/<sid>/<id>, or session/<sid>/<id>. It returns false for bare or malformed addresses.

symbols.does: Emits a decision_take mark after validating the decision address.
symbols.description: decide(decisionAddress, optionLabel, by) first calls _isCanonicalDecisionAddr and throws an error if the address is not canonical. On success it emits a gallery:verb event with verb 'annotate', mark_type 'decision_take', value set to optionLabel, is_decision_take true, and optional by field.

symbols.does: Attaches an annotation UI strip to a single element and wires its controls.
symbols.description: makeAnnotatable(el, address) guards against double-binding via dataset.viBound, marks the element as annotatable, sets data-element-id to the address, appends an .annotation-strip with comment, favour, and reaction buttons, and adds click listeners that emit comments, favours, reactions, and visual stamps.

symbols.does: Creates a single comment-type picker panel on the target element.
symbols.description: openCommentPicker(el, address) toggles a .comment-picker panel containing chips for each CONTENT_COMMENT_TYPES value, a textarea, and a send button. The chosen type defaults to 'direction' and the entered text is emitted as a comment mark when sent.

symbols.does: Appends a visual stamp to an element after a reaction is placed.
symbols.description: markPlaced(el, r) creates a span with classes 'placed-stamp' and the reaction name, then sets its innerHTML to the corresponding icon or the reaction text. It provides immediate visual feedback independent of downstream state.

symbols.does: Prompts the user for a numeric favour score.
symbols.description: promptFavour() calls window.prompt with the message 'Favour score (number):' and returns null if cancelled, otherwise returns the input converted with Number(). It is a minimal placeholder input mechanism.

symbols.does: Handles render events and binds annotatable elements under the rendered root.
symbols.description: _onRendered(e) reads detail.elements, detail.element, or detail.root, selects anchorable elements using detail.anchorableSelector, [data-anchorable], or a semantic fallback, and calls makeAnnotatable for each with a derived sub-address. It warns when zero elements are bound. It is registered on both 'gallery:rendered' and 'decision:rendered'.
